steps.text: Hey! You made it. I'm Evan - I run this place. That the birthday PixBot? Solid starter frame. Corp sponsors our trainer table so new pilots can learn without scratching the paint. Hop on, I'll walk you through it.

This is your bot's guts - the hex grid. Core makes energy, tiles route it and flavor it, weapon mounts throw it at the other guy. Every PixBot in this shop runs on that one idea, including mine. Mine's just prettier.

See those tabs up top? Torso, both arms, both legs, head, backpack - each one's its OWN hex grid, and yeah, you gotta build every one you want to matter. Torso's the heart: Core Reactor lives there, plus a little sink tile for every limb. Skip a limb's sink and that limb just never sees power - I've watched pilots swear their arm cannon is broken for a week before somebody points that out.

Let's build this properly instead of me just talking at you. I'll point at exactly where each piece goes, given what's actually in your bin - grab the tile I call out and drop it on the glowing hex. If it needs pointing somewhere too, I'll show you that next.
Torso's live. Now click the 'L. Arm' tab up top - let's get you a weapon.

Now hit 'Simulate Energy Flow' and watch the juice actually move through what you built. Honestly the best part of the hobby. I've got regulars who never take their bots off the bench - they just sit here rerouting packets all afternoon.

Simulating shows you where the energy GOES. Test Range shows you what it actually DOES - real shots, real spread, real damage numbers, fired at a dummy that won't shoot back. Use it before you trust a build with your life out there.

Quick tip before your first match: route power through an Accumulator and it banks a BIG shot on keys 1, 2, or 3. Your regular trigger keeps working - at half strength while the bank fills, full once it's ready. Press the key, watch the fireworks. The kids call it the nuke button. I also call it the nuke button.

Bots take real hits out there. A bad one can fry a tile dead - it stays dead until you repair it back here, and repairs cost scrap. Sell me your junk tiles anytime, I'm not picky. The scrap economy is half the game, kid.

One more before I let you loose: infuse a chip you've extracted into ANOTHER tile and that tile runs hot on that stat - stack a few and it really cooks. And that case in the back is the Black Market: rotating oversized experimental gear, no refunds, restock every ten minutes. Corp doesn't officially endorse it. I don't officially discourage it.

Now - about the house bots. The Corp's trainer AI learns. Every match you play, it studies you, breeds new squads, holds a little grudge. Hit TAB anytime to see the War Room - that's its homework on YOU. Know your enemy. It absolutely knows you.

U pops the minimap, mouse wheel pulls the camera back so you can see the whole table. Red dots are trouble. Green dot is the way home. You'll figure out the rest by getting shot at, which is the traditional method.

That's the whole loop: build, brawl, salvage, rebuild better. Your bot's registered with the Corp now - win matches, earn parts, and maybe someday it's YOUR name on the tournament bracket out front. Door's always open. Except after 10:00 PM Weeknights, and Tuesdays after 4 PM Now go make some noise.
